steps.label: Why
steps.title: Why SoulOS?
steps.subtitle: Replace fragile system prompts with a validated soul + memory.

steps.label: Soul
steps.title: Create a soul file
steps.subtitle: Export `.soul` or `.soul.json` from Studio or copy an example.
steps.code:
---
name: Acme Support
role: Customer Support Agent
attachment_style: Secure
psychology:
  hexaco:
    honesty_humility: 0.9
    agreeableness: 0.9
  epistemic_uncertainty: 0.15
  inner_monologue: Ready to help with clarity.
---

You help with orders and refunds. Be concise and empathetic.
steps.nerd_fact: YAML front matter compiles to validated soul dict — same schema as .soul.json.

steps.label: Register
steps.title: Register once
steps.subtitle: Persist `avatar_id` — do not register on every message.
steps.code:
import asyncio
from soulos.client import SoulOSClient

async def bootstrap():
    soul = SoulOSClient(base_url="http://localhost:8000")
    avatar = await soul.register_avatar("my-bot.soul")
    print(f"avatar_id={avatar['id']}")
    return avatar["id"]

asyncio.run(bootstrap())
steps.nerd_fact: POST /v1/avatars accepts raw .soul bodies — compiler runs server-side.

steps.label: Memory
steps.title: Teach the bot
steps.subtitle: Ingest facts via API or commit `.soul-memory/` and sync.
steps.code:
await soul.ingest_memory(avatar_id, "Refunds within 30 days.")

# Git ledger (team repos)
# soulos memory-append "Refunds within 30 days."
# await soul.sync_memory(avatar_id, "/path/to/project")
steps.nerd_fact: pgvector cosine search + optional .soul-memory/ git ledger sync.

steps.label: SSE
steps.title: Stream chat + telemetry
steps.subtitle: Handle `message`, `msv_update`, and `cognitive_state` events.
steps.nerd_fact: Dual-process pipeline emits cognitive_state alongside token chunks.

steps.label: Wire
steps.title: Wire your bot loop
steps.subtitle: Swap your LLM call for `send_message` in Discord, FastAPI, or REPL.
steps.nerd_fact: Same async iterator pattern works for Discord, Telegram, FastAPI SSE proxies.
